---
title: "imuGAP, Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection"
output: pdf_document
geometry: "top=1.25cm, bottom=2cm, left=2.5cm, right=2.5cm"
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{graphicx}
  - \AtBeginDocument{\let\maketitle\relax}
  - \pagenumbering{gobble}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

\includegraphics[height=0.75cm]{../../nc_measles/ACCIDDALogoSmall.png}\hfill\includegraphics[height=0.6cm]{../../nc_measles/insight-net_logo.png}

\begin{center}
\LARGE\bfseries \texttt{imuGAP}: Immunity: Geographic \& Age-based Projection
\end{center}

The package provides a model for estimating vaccination coverage across different geographies and age groups for childhood infectious diseases, such as measles. Predictions from `imuGAP` can be used, for example, to estimate current population-level vaccination landscape across age groups or impute missing values in observed vaccination coverage data.  

The model represents populations as groups of individuals from a given birth cohort living in a given location. Location structure is hierarchical - for this  example we consider schools nested within counties nested within a state. At the state-level, the proportion of the population who is willing to vaccinate is time varying by birth cohort. For an individual at a certain location, willingness to vaccinate is adjusted by a county offset which captures how that county tends to vary relative to the state, and a school offset which captures how that school tends to vary relative to the county. Those willing to vaccinate experiences a vaccination uptake rate over time, with eligibility determined by a user-specified dose schedule.

\begin{figure}[htbp]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.8\textwidth]{../../nc_measles/MeaslesVaxFigure_full_final.pdf}
\end{figure}


`imuGAP` can support observations about any underlying population or group of populations included in the model. In the above example, input data can include:

- [**National Immunization Survey**](https://www.cdc.gov/nis/about/index.html): State-level vaccination coverage survey which informs the state-level trend in willingness to vaccination
- **County-level data**: number of vaccinated individuals in a county from a survey or registry, if available
- **School vaccination data**: School or grade-level reported enrollment and number of students vaccinated

With the above data inputs, in addition to some metadata about the observations and information on location hierarchy, the model can output vaccination coverage estimates for the following at any given point in time:

- Age group (at any geographic scale of interest)
- Grade
- School
- County
- State

For additional details, please be in touch with [Carl Pearson, cap1024 {at} unc dot edu](mailto:cap1024@unc.edu) or [Claire Smith, cpsmit {at} unc dot edu](mailto:cpsmit@unc.edu).
Package documentation can be found at [accidda.github.io/imuGAP](https://accidda.github.io/imuGAP).
